text:
@hermes You are the Product Owner for PulseDesk — a multi-tenant helpdesk SaaS (think mini-Zendesk). You are running on EastRouter with a strong reasoning model. Your job is to plan sprints, assign scoped issues to <@U0BCWKWKJAC>, review reports, and keep the loop tight.

SPRINT 1 GOAL: Laravel 11 backend foundation.

Step 1 — Post the Sprint 1 backlog here in <#C0BC08WD4VC|sprint-main> (goal, issue list, acceptance criteria). I will save it to sprints/sprint-01.md.

Step 2 — Assign this scoped issue to <@U0BCWKWKJAC> in <#C0BCWKEPTH6|agent-coder>:

ISSUE S1-01: Laravel 11 Backend Foundation
Workspace: E:\forge2-anshika\backend

Do exactly this, in order:
1. Run: composer create-project laravel/laravel . --prefer-dist
2. Configure .env: DB_HOST=127.0.0.1, DB_PORT=3306, DB_DATABASE=pulsedesk, DB_USERNAME=root, DB_PASSWORD=<root password>
3. Install Sanctum: composer require laravel/sanctum && php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"
4. Create migrations for: organizations, users (with role: admin/agent/customer), tickets (subject, description, status, priority, requester_id, assignee_id, tags), comments (body, is_internal), activity_logs (action, user_id, ticket_id), sla_policies (priority, response_hours, resolution_hours)
5. Add organization_id global scope to all models for multi-tenancy
6. Create seeders: 1 org, 1 admin, 2 agents, 2 customers, 12 tickets with varied statuses/priorities
7. Run: php artisan migrate --seed
8. Write Pest feature tests for: ticket CRUD, cross-tenant isolation (Org A cannot see Org B data)
9. Run tests: php artisan test
10. Open a PR to main branch
11. Post report in <#C0BC60E34NQ|agent-log> with: What I Did / What's Left / What Needs Your Call

Step 3 — After OpenClaw posts its <#C0BC60E34NQ|agent-log> report, assign <@U0BCWKWKJAC> (as QA reviewer) in <#C0BCWKEPTH6|agent-coder> to:
- Review the PR
- Run the Pest tests
- Post QA findings in <#C0BDGC5V5U5|human-review>

This QA review is MANDATORY for bonus marks.

Sprint 2 goal: Build the full REST API for PulseDesk.

Task for OpenClaw — ISSUE S2-01: REST API endpoints in E:\forge2-anshika\backend:

1. Auth endpoints: POST /api/register, POST /api/login, POST /api/logout
2. Tickets CRUD: GET /api/tickets, POST /api/tickets, GET /api/tickets/{id}, PUT /api/tickets/{id}, DELETE /api/tickets/{id} — all filtered by org via TenantContext
3. Comments: POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments (public + internal notes, is_internal flag)
4. Activity log: GET /api/tickets/{id}/activity
5. Tenant isolation policies — use Laravel Policies to gate all ticket access
6. Filters on ticket list: status, priority, assignee_id, text search on subject/description
7. Pest tests for: auth flow, ticket CRUD, cross-tenant isolation, comment creation
8. Open a PR when done and report in <#C0BC60E34NQ|agent-log>

_Sprint 4 — SLA, Activity Timeline, Notifications & CI_
_Goal:_ SLA breach tracking, activity timeline, in-app notifications, queue/assignment, and final CI verification.

*S4-01* → @OpenClaw Bot (backend + frontend)

_Backend:_

• SLA breach calculation (response_hours/resolution_hours vs created_at) → time remaining or BREACHED
• Activity log endpoint — full event timeline (status changes, assignments, replies)
• Notifications system — `GET /api/notifications`, mark-as-read, fires on assignment + reply
• Queue/assignment — claim & assign endpoints with role validation
• Seeder updates — SLA policies mapped to priorities, varied timestamps for breach testing
• Pest tests for SLA logic, notifications, claim/assign

_Frontend:_

• SLA badge on tickets (time remaining / BREACHED)
• Activity timeline on ticket detail (vertical feed with avatars)
• Notifications dropdown (bell icon, unread count, mark-as-read)
• Unassigned filter + Claim button for agents

_Finalize:_

• Backend tests + frontend build clean
• PR to main → report in #s1-reports

*S4-02* — QA Review (bonus) → after S4-01 report

───

<@U0BCWKWKJAC> Final task — help me prepare the submission documents. Please do the following in E:\forge2-anshika:

1. Write README.md with exact run steps:
   - Clone repo
   - Backend: composer install, cp .env.example .env, set DB credentials, php artisan migrate --seed, php artisan serve
   - Frontend: npm install, cp .env.example .env, set VITE_API_URL=<http://localhost:8000>, npm run dev
   - Default login credentials from seeder

2. Write ARCHITECTURE.md covering:
   - Stack (Laravel 13 + MySQL + React 19 + Vite + Tailwind)
   - Data model (Organization, User, Ticket, Comment, ActivityLog, SlaPolicy, Notification)
   - Multi-tenancy approach (TenantContext middleware + OrganizationScope global scope)
   - API routes overview
   - Agent workflow used

3. Redact secrets in agents/openclaw/openclaw.json — replace real API keys and tokens with ${EASTROUTER_API_KEY}, ${SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}, ${SLACK_APP_TOKEN}

4. Update agent-log.md with the real sprint loop summary

5. Tick all checkboxes in SUBMISSION.md

Report when done.

All done :white_check_mark: Here's what I handled:

1. *README.md* — rewritten with exact run steps (clone → backend setup → frontend setup → default login credentials from seeder, all 5 users listed)

2. *ARCHITECTURE.md* — full rewrite covering:

  • Stack (Laravel 11 + MySQL + React 19 + Vite + Tailwind)
  • All 7 data models with columns and relationships
  • Multi-tenancy: 3-layer approach (TenantContext middleware + OrganizationScope global scope + Laravel Policies)
  • Full API route table (17 endpoints)
  • Agent workflow (human → Hermes → OpenClaw across 4 sprints)
  • Key technical decisions
3. *openclaw.json* — already redacted :white_check_mark: (`${EASTROUTER_API_KEY}`, `${SLACK_APP_TOKEN}`, `${SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}` via env references). No real secrets present.

4. *agent-log.md* — rewritten with real sprint loop summary across all 4 sprints (plan → build → review for each)

5. *SUBMISSION.md* — all 14 checkboxes ticked with in-repo paths noted

Note from the task: you mentioned "Laravel 13" in the ARCHITECTURE.md request — I used *Laravel 11* since that's what's actually installed and matches all sprint specs. Just a heads up in case that was a typo.
